title: TaxMappingRules
description: Schema for the workbench-ynab payee/category → tax-line mapping ruleset (issue #23, M3-4). A ruleset is an ordered list of rules; the classifier (lib/tax/classifyTransaction.mjs) evaluates them against an already-fetched YNAB transaction and returns the highest-priority match. This schema is GENERIC and shareable: nothing here may be specific to one taxpayer. Real business names, account names, and category-group labels live ONLY in the user's profile/rules instance (issue #25), never in the bundled defaults (assets/tax/mapping-rules.json).
$comment: MONEY UNITS: a rule's amountThresholdDollars is in US DOLLARS, not YNAB milliunits. YNAB returns transaction amounts in milliunits (1000 milliunits = $1); the classifier divides the transaction's milliunit amount by 1000 BEFORE comparing it to amountThresholdDollars or deriving amountSign. taxLineId references the bundled tax-line catalog (assets/tax/us-tax-lines.json, issue #21): e.g. schedC.27a, schedA.medical, schedA.salt, schedA.interest, schedA.charitable. The reason field is a template: {payee}, {categoryName}, {categoryGroup}, {accountName}, {taxLineId}, {businessEntityId}, and {matchedKeyword} are substituted at classify time. A businessEntityId of "$profile" is a SENTINEL, not a real id: the classifier resolves it to the owning business entity from the profile (the entity whose declared business category-groups/accounts the transaction belongs to, or — for non-structural matches — the sole business entity when exactly one exists). This keeps owner-specific entity ids out of the bundled defaults (issue #23 AC).

$schema.description: Optional pointer to this schema, so editors can validate an instance. Annotation only.

$comment.description: Optional inline documentation for the ruleset file (matches the convention used by us-tax-lines.json). Annotation only.

rulesetVersion.description: Version of this ruleset shape, so future migrations can detect and upgrade older bundled/user rulesets.

rules.description: Ordered list of mapping rules. Evaluation order is governed by each rule's priority (ascending) with documented tie-breaking, NOT array position — see docs/mapping-engine.md.

rule.description: A single mapping rule. A rule matches a transaction when EVERY criterion present in its match object matches (logical AND). A rule must declare at least one criterion.

id.description: Stable, opaque rule identifier. A user rule whose id equals a bundled rule's id REPLACES that bundled rule (so users disable, re-prioritize, or patch a default by id without editing repo files); a user rule with a new id is appended.

match.description: Match criteria. At least one must be present; all present criteria are ANDed. Match-type precedence (strongest signal first), used for tie-breaking among equal-priority rules: categoryName > categoryGroup > accountName > payeeKeywords. The structural businessSignal matcher is treated at the categoryGroup/accountName strength tier.

payeeKeywords.description: Match if ANY keyword matches the transaction payee (case-insensitive). A plain string is a case-insensitive substring match; a string wrapped in slashes ("/aws|gcp/") is a case-insensitive regular expression (the i flag is always added).

categoryName.description: Case-insensitive EXACT match against the transaction's YNAB category name. Strongest signal.

categoryGroup.description: Case-insensitive EXACT match against the transaction's YNAB category-group name.

accountName.description: Case-insensitive EXACT match against the transaction's YNAB account name.

amountSign.description: Match the direction of money flow. YNAB convention: an outflow (expense) is a NEGATIVE amount, an inflow (income/refund) is POSITIVE. The classifier reads the sign of the dollar amount (milliunits / 1000); the sign is unchanged by the conversion.

amountThresholdDollars.description: Match when the transaction's ABSOLUTE dollar magnitude is at least this many DOLLARS. The classifier divides the YNAB milliunit amount by 1000 before comparing — this field is dollars, never milliunits.

businessSignal.description: Structural matcher. When true, the rule matches only if the transaction posts to a business category-group or business account declared by some profile business entity (entity.scheduleLineMap.categoryGroups / .accounts). On a match the owning entity scopes a "$profile" businessEntityId. Encodes the prototype's "transaction in a business account or business category-group is a likely Schedule C expense" signal without naming any entity.

taxLineId.description: Tax-line id this rule classifies to, referencing the bundled catalog (assets/tax/us-tax-lines.json, issue #21), e.g. schedC.27a or schedA.medical.

businessEntityId.description: Optional Schedule C scoping. A literal entity id scopes to that entity; the sentinel "$profile" defers resolution to the classifier (the entity owning the matched business category-group/account, or the sole business entity for non-structural matches). MUST NOT be a real, owner-specific id in the bundled defaults.

priority.description: Evaluation precedence, ASCENDING: the lowest priority number is evaluated first and the first matching rule wins. Lower number = stronger.

confidence.description: Confidence in [0,1] returned with a match, so the review skill can decide what to auto-suggest versus flag for human approval. Defaults to 0.5 when omitted.

enabled.description: Whether this rule participates in evaluation. Defaults to true. A user disables a bundled default by overlaying a rule with the same id and enabled:false.

reason.description: Human-readable explanation template returned with a match. Supports {payee}, {categoryName}, {categoryGroup}, {accountName}, {taxLineId}, {businessEntityId}, and {matchedKeyword} placeholders.
